error.signature: MongoServerError: chunk too large to move: data size exceeds maximum chunk size

verdict.summary: Chunk exceeds maximum size (default 128MB) and cannot be migrated. Caused by poor shard key choice leading to jumbo chunks.

dead_ends.action: Increase chunkSize to accommodate the large chunks
dead_ends.why_fails: Larger chunks reduce balancer effectiveness. The root issue is shard key cardinality, not chunk size.

dead_ends.action: Manually move the jumbo chunk with force flag
dead_ends.why_fails: Force-moving jumbo chunks can cause out-of-memory on the target shard during migration.

workarounds.how: sh.splitAt('mydb.collection', { shardKeyField: splitPoint })  # find splitPoint with db.collection.find().sort({shardKey:1}).skip(N)

workarounds.action: Refine the shard key to increase cardinality (MongoDB 5.0+)
Clear the jumbo flag after manual splitting
workarounds.how: db.adminCommand({ clearJumboFlag: 'mydb.collection', bounds: [{ key: MinKey }, { key: MaxKey }] })
